items.title: African Union peacekeeping mission in Somalia at risk amid US funding cuts
items.description: Washington said its decision was due the government’s lack of progress in containing the al-Shabaab group or in taking ownership of its own security.
items.titleIt: African Union peacekeeping mission in Somalia at risk amid US funding cuts

items.title: Somalia: African Union Calls Emergency Meeting As U.S. Ends Somalia Army Funds
items.description: [Vanguard] The African Union called an emergency meeting on its military mission in Somalia on Friday after Washington announced it would end critical funding over the lack of progress against Islamist insurgents.
items.titleIt: Somalia: African Union Calls Emergency Meeting As U.S. Ends Somalia Army Funds

items.title: Humanitarian Corridors: 27 Refugees from Ethiopia Arrive in Italy
items.description: (ANSA) - NAIROBI, 03 LUG - The process that enabled 27 Somali and Sudanese refugees from Ethiopia to depart for Italy—carried out through the “humanitarian corridors” program—has been successfully completed. The initiative, the result of a memorandum of understanding between the Community of Sant’Egidio, the Ministries of the Interior and of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation, and the Italian Episcopal Conference, represents a significant example of institutional cooperation and international solidarity. The embassy, which has been collaborating with the Community of Sant’Egidio on
items.titleIt: Humanitarian Corridors: 27 Refugees from Ethiopia Arrive in Italy

items.title: US to block UN funding for Somalia in blow to AU peace mission
items.description: In a diplomatic note to the African Union seen by Reuters, the US has vowed to block any spending of UN funds on the AU Support ​and Stabilisation Mission in Somalia (AUSSOM).
items.titleIt: US to block UN funding for Somalia in blow to AU peace mission

items.title: Somalia Honors Italy for Health Cooperation
items.description: (ANSA) - NAIROBI, 03 LUG - The Italian Ambassador to Somalia, Pier Mario Daccò Coppi, received an honorary award from the management of the Forlanini Hospital in Mogadishu—which was recently modernized thanks to funding from Italian Cooperation—at the conclusion of a meeting dedicated to healthcare cooperation between the two countries. During the meeting, the hospital’s general director, Mohamed Jafar Ali, outlined the progress made by the facility, focusing on the healthcare services offered, ongoing development projects, and the creation of new wards, particularly those dedicated to women’s
items.titleIt: Somalia Honors Italy for Health Cooperation

items.title: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran
items.description: Egypt has been walking a tightrope since the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran began, joining mediation efforts while signalling solidarity with Gulf Arab partners under Iranian attack. But with some Gulf capitals voicing expectations of stronger support, Cairo may find itself under increasing pressure.
items.titleIt: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran

items.title: Sudan, South Sudan and Somalia: Key Peace and Security Priorities for Africa in 2026
items.description: In this online event, Crisis Group experts discussed peace and security priorities for the African continent in 2026, zooming in on Somalia, Sudan and South Sudan ahead of the African Union's summit of heads of state.
items.titleIt: Sudan, South Sudan and Somalia: Key Peace and Security Priorities for Africa in 2026

items.title: US-Italy ties excellent, Trump and Meloni have long-standing relationship - Fertitta
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 2 - US-Italy relations are excellent and leaders Donald Trump and Giorgia Meloni have a long-standing relationship, Us Ambassador to Rome Tilman J. Fertitta said after recent strains over the Iran war and the American president's claim the Italian premier begged for a photo at the G7 summit in France. "I would say relations between the US and Italy are excellent," Fertitta said, responding to reporters at the reception celebrating the 250th anniversary of US independence at Villa Taverna. "I deal with everyone every day, from the prime minister on down, and we collaborate on
items.titleIt: US-Italy ties excellent, Trump and Meloni have long-standing relationship - Fertitta

items.title: Ambassador Nicoletti’s Mission to the Argentine Province of Catamarca
items.description: (ANSA) - BUENOS AIRES, 01 LUG - The Italian Ambassador to Argentina, Fabrizio Nicoletti, undertook an institutional mission to the Province of Catamarca on June 29 and 30, with the aim of consolidating dialogue with provincial and municipal authorities and strengthening ties with the historic Italian community in the region. According to a press release, the visit represented an important opportunity to explore opportunities for collaboration between Italy and Catamarca in strategic sectors such as the mining industry, technological innovation, and sustainability. The mission began with a work
items.titleIt: Ambassador Nicoletti’s Mission to the Argentine Province of Catamarca

items.title: Sudan's RSF committed ethnic cleansing, Amnesty report alleges
items.description: Sudan's paramilitary Rapid Support Forces committed crimes against humanity and ethnic cleansing during their campaign to seize El-Fasher between 2024 and 2025, according to a new report from Amnesty International released on Wednesday.
items.titleIt: Sudan's RSF committed ethnic cleansing, Amnesty report alleges

items.title: Italian woman released in Egypt after Tajani intervenes
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 1 - Nessy Guerra, an Italian woman currently obliged to live in Egypt with her three-year-old daughter due to a custody battle with her estranged Egyptian husband, was released on Wednesday following an intervention by Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani after Egyptian police went to her Cairo home and took her to a station overnight, sources said. Guerra has returned home with her daughter, the sources said. The Egyptian police said Guerra had been detained in relation to an order by an Egyptian judge saying the father must have access to the child. Guerra received assistance f
items.titleIt: Italian woman released in Egypt after Tajani intervenes

items.title: Cairo embassy has requested pardon for Nessy Guerra says Tajani
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 1 - Deputy Premier and Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani said Wednesday that Italy's embassy in Cairo has made a formal request to the Egyptian authorities for a presidential pardon for an adultery conviction for Nessy Guerra, an Italian woman currently obliged to live in Egypt with her three-year-old daughter due to a custody battle with her estranged Egyptian husband. During a question time session in the Lower House, the minister confirmed that he intervened personally, speaking to his Egyptian counterpart for Guerra's release after she was taken from her home in Cairo over
items.titleIt: Cairo embassy has requested pardon for Nessy Guerra says Tajani

items.title: Working to bring Nessy Guerra and daughter back to Italy says Tajani
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 1 - The government is working to make it possible for Nessy Guerra, an Italian woman currently obliged to live in Egypt with her three-year-old daughter due to a custody battle with her estranged Egyptian husband, to return to Italy, Deputy Premier and Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani said on Wednesday. Tajani commented on the case via X following his intervention to have Guerra released after she was taken from her home in Cairo overnight and held at a police station. items.titleIt: Working to bring Nessy Guerra and daughter back to Italy says Tajani
